source_pr.title: schematracker: treat TableInfo as immutable
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: lance6716 <lance6716@gmail.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #35933

Problem Summary:

before this PR, DM needs to clone the TableInfo after reading in order to avoid data race with SchemaTracker, but that consumes much memory. After this PR TableInfo is immutable so no need to clone it.

### What is changed and how it works?

before SchemaTracker will modify a TableInfo, it will clone it first. After the modification, it will put it to InfoStore. 

### Check List

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Data race risk between DM reading `TableInfo` and SchemaTracker mutating the same in-memory metadata; readers needed to clone defensively to avoid races.
failure_signature: Potential DATA RACE on shared `TableInfo`/schema metadata between readers and SchemaTracker mutations.
root_cause_explanation: The issue was caused by shared mutable metadata: SchemaTracker stored `DBInfo`/`TableInfo` pointers that could later be mutated in place, so other components (like DM) reading the same objects could race with SchemaTracker updates. This PR treats stored metadata as immutable by cloning `DBInfo`/`TableInfo` before applying DDL changes and then writing the cloned copy back, avoiding in-place mutation and reducing reader-side cloning needs.

evidence: evidence: before this PR, DM needs to clone the TableInfo after reading in order to avoid data race with SchemaTracker, but that consumes much memory. After this PR TableInfo is immutable so no need to clone it.
